informal: Let $G$ be a group of order 105. Prove that if a Sylow 3-subgroup of $G$ is normal then $G$ is abelian.
lean:
theorem Dummit_Foote_exercise_4_5_28 {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G]
  (hG : card G = 105) (P : Sylow 3 G) [hP : P.Normal] :
  ∀ a b : G, a*b = b*a
reasoning: The informal statement says: if G has order 105 and a Sylow 3-subgroup is normal, then G is abelian. The Lean signature assumes G is a finite group with card 105, takes a Sylow 3-subgroup P with P.Normal, and concludes ∀ a b : G, a*b = b*a, which is exactly the definition of G being abelian. The hypothesis "P : Sylow 3 G" with [hP : P.Normal] correctly encodes "a Sylow 3-subgroup of G is normal" (existence of a normal Sylow 3-subgroup; since all Sylow 3-subgroups are conjugate, one being normal is equivalent to the unique one being normal). This matches the informal statement faithfully.
